description:
responsibility of this directory
- This directory contains classes that implement the ValueAggregator interface.
- These classes are used to aggregate values based on their type and id.
- The classes in this directory collaborate by calling each other's methods to perform the aggregation.
- The architectural role or pattern of this directory is to provide a modular and extensible way to perform value aggregation.

how the files or child directories collaborate
- The classes in this directory collaborate by calling each other's methods to perform the aggregation.
- The DoubleValueSum class aggregates a sequence of double values, while the LongValueMax class maintains the maximum of a sequence of long values.
- The LongValueMin class maintains the minimum of a sequence of long values, and the LongValueSum class aggregates a sequence of long values.
- The StringValueMax class maintains the biggest of a sequence of strings, and the StringValueMin class aggregates the smallest string value from a sequence of strings.
- The UniqValueCount class deduplicates a sequence of objects, and the ValueHistogram class computes the histogram of a sequence of strings.

the architectural role or pattern of this directory
- The classes in this directory implement the ValueAggregator interface, which provides a common interface for value aggregation.
- This allows for easy extension and customization of the aggregation functionality.
- The use of the ValueAggregator interface promotes code reuse and modularity.
- The directory follows the principles of the decorator pattern, where each class decorates the functionality of the ValueAggregator interface.
